root_text.iti42:2.4: Ime ce, bhikkhave, dve sukkā dhammā lokaṁ na pāleyyuṁ, nayidha paññāyetha mātāti vā mātucchāti vā mātulānīti vā ācariyabhariyāti vā garūnaṁ dārāti vā.
root_text.iti42:2.5: Sambhedaṁ loko agamissa yathā ajeḷakā kukkuṭasūkarā soṇasiṅgālā.
root_text.iti42:2.6: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, ime dve sukkā dhammā lokaṁ pālenti tasmā paññāyati mātāti vā mātucchāti vā mātulānīti vā ācariyabhariyāti vā garūnaṁ dārāti vā”ti.
root_text.iti42:5.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti42:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti42:2.1: “These two bright things, mendicants, protect the world.
translation_text.iti42:2.4: If these two bright things did not protect the world, there would be no recognition of the status of mother, aunts, or wives and partners of tutors and respected people.
translation_text.iti42:2.5: The world would become dissolute, like goats and sheep, chickens and pigs, and hounds and jackals.
translation_text.iti42:2.6: But because the two bright things protect the world, there is recognition of the status of mother, aunts, and wives and partners of tutors and respected people.”
translation_text.iti42:3.4: and fare on in birth and death.
translation_text.iti42:4.1: Those in whom conscience and prudence
translation_text.iti42:4.4: being at peace, they will not be reborn again.”
translation_text.iti42:5.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
